# SC-15: Adversarial AI Robustness Testing

**Track:** Security Controls (`SC`) · **Priority:** COULD (optional) · **Status:** draft

For AI components: test resilience against adversarial attacks targeting the model and its inference endpoints. Test categories: prompt injection attacks (malicious instructions to LLMs), data poisoning (training data corruption), model poisoning (compromise of pre-trained model components), adversarial examples (crafted inputs to degrade accuracy), confidentiality probes (attempts to extract training data or model weights), and API-level attacks on inference endpoints. AC-05 covers bias/fairness; this control covers the security-oriented attack surface of AI systems per EU AI Act Art. 15(5).

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 4 | Testing & Documentation | Conditional | *Adversarial AI testing is specialised and high-effort. Recommended for high-risk or security-critical AI deployments. Phase 3 capability.*

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Agent executes adversarial tests, human reviews findings |
| **Agent does** | Execute adversarial robustness tests: prompt injection fuzzing, input perturbation testing, model extraction attacks, membership inference probes. Generate adversarial examples using techniques (FGSM, PGD, C&W). Measure accuracy degradation. Report findings. |
| **Human does** | Review adversarial test findings. Determine: is the accuracy degradation acceptable? Are inference endpoints sufficiently hardened? Request design changes (input validation, rate limiting, model hardening) if robustness is insufficient. |

## Inputs

| Artifact | Source Control | Description |
|----------|---------------|-------------|
| Trained AI model | Model ready for testing |  |
| Test data | Valid and adversarial test inputs |  |
| Model metadata | AC-04 | Model limitations and known vulnerabilities |


## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| Adversarial Robustness Test Report | report | Findings by adversarial attack category: prompt injection success rate, adversarial example success rate, accuracy degradation, confidentiality breach attempts. Recommendations for model hardening or input validation. | 7 years (audit trail) |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_release |
| **SLA** | 480 min |
| **Tooling** | Adversarial attack frameworks (Foolbox, CleverHans, TextAttack), Model security testing (Giskard, AI Fairness 360), Prompt injection testing tools |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | escalate |
| **Escalate to** | Chief Information Security Officer |

If adversarial tests reveal unacceptable degradation in model accuracy or successful model extraction attacks, escalate to CISO for risk decision.

## Dependencies

- `AC-03`
- `AC-05`


## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 9(2) | Protection of ICT systems from unauthorised access and manipulation |
| EU AI Act | Art. 15(5) | Protection against data poisoning, model poisoning, adversarial examples targeting accuracy, confidentiality breaches, and model flaws |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Security · **Owner role:** AI Security Researcher
